seoTitle: Manually Refresh Your Data in Power BI Desktop: Basics
metaDescription: Learn how to manually refresh data in Power BI Desktop, ensuring your reports always reflect the most current information available.
intro: Sometimes you need your Power BI report updated right now rather than waiting for a scheduled refresh. This guide covers how to manually refresh your data in Power BI Desktop, verify the update, and understand when this basic skill matters most.

steps.heading: Access the manual refresh option
steps.body: Locate the refresh button within Power BI Desktop, which triggers an immediate update of your report's underlying data.

steps.heading: Execute the data refresh
steps.body: Click refresh and allow Power BI to pull the latest data from your connected sources into the report.

steps.heading: Verify your data updates
steps.body: Check your visuals after the refresh completes to confirm the data has updated correctly and reflects the latest values.

steps.heading: Understand key business use cases
steps.body: Manual refresh is useful in scenarios like adjusting a marketing campaign based on the latest numbers or reviewing recent customer information before a meeting.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for data accuracy
steps.body: Always confirm your refresh completed successfully before relying on the report for decision-making, especially ahead of important presentations.

faq.question: How is a manual refresh different from a scheduled refresh?
faq.answer: A manual refresh updates your data immediately on demand, while a scheduled refresh runs automatically at set intervals without you needing to trigger it.

faq.question: Does a manual refresh require an internet connection?
faq.answer: Yes, if your data sources are cloud-based or external, an internet connection is needed to pull the latest information during a refresh.

faq.question: Why might a manual refresh fail in Power BI Desktop?
faq.answer: Common causes include connectivity issues, changed data source structures, or expired credentials for a connected source.

faq.question: Is manually refreshing data necessary if I have scheduled refresh set up?
faq.answer: Not usually, but it's useful when you need the very latest data immediately rather than waiting for the next scheduled update.
